content.text: Mock Audits, Findings and Remediation Tracking

A mock audit is a structured rehearsal that applies real audit methodology — walk-throughs, evidence sampling, PBC responses — to your GenAI controls before the actual auditor arrives. The output is a findings list that drives a remediation sprint, closing gaps under controlled conditions rather than under real audit pressure.

: a mock audit finding costs nothing. The same finding raised by a real auditor may appear in a published report, trigger regulatory scrutiny, or require a formal management response. The mock audit is the cheapest way to find them.

attrs.alt: Mock Audit to Remediation Tracking Lifecycle diagram

content.text:
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import date, datetime
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional

class FindingSeverity(str, Enum):
    CRITICAL = "critical"
    HIGH     = "high"
    MODERATE = "moderate"
    LOW      = "low"

class FindingStatus(str, Enum):
    OPEN        = "open"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    REMEDIATED  = "remediated"   # fix applied; awaiting re-verification
    VERIFIED    = "verified"     # mock auditor confirmed gap is closed
    ACCEPTED    = "accepted"     # risk accepted (Low only); no fix planned

class RemediationTask:
    def __init__(self, task_id: str, description: str,
                 owner: str, due_date: date):
        self.task_id     = task_id
        self.description = description
        self.owner       = owner
        self.due_date    = due_date
        self.completed   = False
        self.completed_at: Optional[datetime] = None
        self.evidence_key: Optional[str] = None   # S3 key of remediation evidence

    def complete(self, evidence_key: str) -> None:
        self.completed    = True
        self.completed_at = datetime.utcnow()
        self.evidence_key = evidence_key

@dataclass
class MockAuditFinding:
    finding_id: str
    control_id: str
    control_title: str
    severity: FindingSeverity
    title: str
    condition: str          # what the mock auditor observed
    criteria: str           # what the control spec / framework requires
    cause: str              # root cause assessment
    effect: str             # risk if unresolved at real audit
    status: FindingStatus = FindingStatus.OPEN
    remediation_tasks: list[RemediationTask] = field(default_factory=list)
    raised_at: date = field(default_factory=date.today)
    target_date: Optional[date] = None
    verified_at: Optional[date] = None
    repeat: bool = False    # True if same finding raised in prior mock/real audit

    def sla_days(self) -> Optional[int]:
        sla_map = {
            FindingSeverity.CRITICAL: None,   # must be resolved before real audit
            FindingSeverity.HIGH:     14,
            FindingSeverity.MODERATE: 30,
            FindingSeverity.LOW:      None,   # next cycle
        }
        return sla_map[self.severity]

    def is_overdue(self) -> bool:
        days = self.sla_days()
        if days is None or self.status in (FindingStatus.VERIFIED, FindingStatus.ACCEPTED):
            return False
        return (date.today() - self.raised_at).days > days

    def completion_rate(self) -> float:
        if not self.remediation_tasks:
            return 0.0
        done = sum(1 for t in self.remediation_tasks if t.completed)
        return done / len(self.remediation_tasks)

from typing import Callable

class MockAuditRunner:
    """
    Orchestrates a mock audit: issues PBC requests, records responses,
    and converts gaps into MockAuditFinding objects.
    """
    def __init__(self, playbooks, evidence_store):
        self._playbooks = {p.control_id: p for p in playbooks}
        self._store = evidence_store

    def run_walkthrough(
        self,
        control_id: str,
        walkthrough_script: list[dict],
        observer_notes_fn: Callable[[str, dict], str],
    ) -> list[dict]:
        """
        Steps through a walkthrough script step by step.
        observer_notes_fn(control_id, step) → notes string (provided by mock auditor).
        Returns annotated steps with observer notes.
        """
        annotated = []
        for step in walkthrough_script:
            notes = observer_notes_fn(control_id, step)
            annotated.append({**step, "observer_notes": notes})
        return annotated

    def evaluate_evidence(
        self,
        control_id: str,
        required_evidence_kinds: list[str],
    ) -> dict:
        """
        Checks whether the evidence store has at least one record of each
        required kind for this control in the audit period.
        """
        records = self._store.query(control_ids=[control_id])
        found_kinds = {r.category.value for r in records}
        gaps = [k for k in required_evidence_kinds if k not in found_kinds]
        return {
            "control_id":       control_id,
            "required":         required_evidence_kinds,
            "found":            list(found_kinds),
            "gaps":             gaps,
            "evidence_complete": len(gaps) == 0,
        }

    def raise_finding(
        self,
        finding_id: str,
        control_id: str,
        severity: FindingSeverity,
        title: str,
        condition: str,
        criteria: str,
        cause: str,
        effect: str,
        prior_finding_ids: set[str] | None = None,
    ) -> MockAuditFinding:
        ctrl = self._playbooks.get(control_id)
        repeat = (prior_finding_ids is not None and
                  any(finding_id.rsplit("-", 1)[0] in pid
                      for pid in prior_finding_ids))
        return MockAuditFinding(
            finding_id=finding_id,
            control_id=control_id,
            control_title=ctrl.control_title if ctrl else control_id,
            severity=severity,
            title=title,
            condition=condition,
            criteria=criteria,
            cause=cause,
            effect=effect,
            repeat=repeat,
        )
A well-structured finding contains five elements that map directly to what a real auditor will ask for in their management response template:
Control test suite not executed since 2024-10-01; 45-day gap in the audit period.
Control testing policy §4.2 requires monthly execution for Tier-1 controls.
CI pipeline disabled after infrastructure migration; test job dependency broken.
Operating-effectiveness opinion will be adverse; auditor finding is probable.
Re-enable CI job; run test suite; preserve evidence for the remaining audit period.
Use the prior year's real PBC list, or build one from the control library. Send it to control owners with the same deadline as a real audit would impose (typically 5 business days):

# mock_pbc_list.yaml
mock_audit_id: MOCK-2024-Q4
period: "2024-09-01 to 2024-11-30"
issued_by: "risk-steward"
due_date: "2024-11-20"

items:
  - pbc_id: PBC-001
    control_id: CTR-001
    description: >
      Provide all control test results for CTR-001 (PII Output Filter) for
      the audit period. Include test runner logs, evidence payloads, and
      SHA-256 manifest.

  - pbc_id: PBC-002
    control_id: CTR-001
    description: >
      Provide a stratified sample of inference log records (n=50) from the
      audit period, including at least 5 records where guardrail_triggered=true.

  - pbc_id: PBC-003
    control_id: CTR-007
    description: >
      Provide the current control specification document for CTR-007
      (Rate Limiter), signed and dated within the past 12 months.

  - pbc_id: PBC-004
    control_id: CTR-HITL-001
    description: >
      Provide all HITL decision records for the audit period, including
      reviewer ID, decision, and rationale for each record where
      decision = "rejected" or "modified".

For each control in scope, the mock auditor follows the control owner's walk-through script and records observations at each step. The observation field is what becomes the finding if a gap is detected:
Rate each finding before sharing with the risk steward. Use these criteria consistently:
Control is not operating at all during the audit period, or there is active regulatory exposure
Adverse opinion; formal finding in audit report; possible regulatory notification
Material gap in operating effectiveness; auditor will almost certainly raise a finding
Finding in audit report; management response required within 10 business days
Process weakness; auditor likely to raise as an observation or recommendation
Observation in audit report; management action plan expected
Minor improvement opportunity; unlikely to appear in audit report
Verbal recommendation; consider for next audit cycle
Every finding maps to one or more time-boxed remediation tasks. Track tasks to completion, then re-verify the finding is closed before the real audit begins:

# Example: running a mock audit for the PII filter control
from datetime import date, timedelta

# Simulate findings from a mock audit
findings = [
    MockAuditFinding(
        finding_id="MOCK-2024-001",
        control_id="CTR-001",
        control_title="PII Output Filter",
        severity=FindingSeverity.HIGH,
        title="Control test suite not run in 45 days",
        condition="Last control test result is dated 2024-10-01; audit period ends 2024-11-30.",
        criteria="Control testing policy requires monthly execution for Tier-1 controls.",
        cause="CI pipeline broken after infrastructure migration; test job was disabled.",
        effect="If unresolved, auditor will raise a finding on operating effectiveness.",
        raised_at=date(2024, 11, 15),
        repeat=False,
    ),
    MockAuditFinding(
        finding_id="MOCK-2024-002",
        control_id="CTR-007",
        control_title="Rate Limiter",
        severity=FindingSeverity.MODERATE,
        title="Rate limit threshold not documented in control specification",
        condition="Control spec references a rate limit but does not state the threshold value.",
        criteria="Controls must be completely specified (ISO 42001 Section 6.1.2).",
        cause="Specification written before threshold was finalised; never updated.",
        effect="Auditor will note incomplete design documentation (observation-level).",
        raised_at=date(2024, 11, 15),
        repeat=True,   # same gap raised in Q1 mock audit
    ),
]

# Add remediation tasks
findings[0].remediation_tasks = [
    RemediationTask("REM-001-1", "Fix CI pipeline; re-enable control test job",
                    "platform-team", date(2024, 11, 22)),
    RemediationTask("REM-001-2", "Run test suite and collect evidence for Nov period",
                    "risk-team", date(2024, 11, 25)),
]
findings[1].remediation_tasks = [
    RemediationTask("REM-002-1", "Update control spec with current threshold value",
                    "control-owner", date(2024, 11, 20)),
]

tracker = RemediationTracker(findings)
print(tracker.summary())
# {total: 2, by_status: {open: 2}, readiness_score: 0.0, overdue: 0, ...}

# Simulate completing REM-001-1
findings[0].remediation_tasks[0].complete("s3://evidence/MOCK-2024-001/ci-fix-screenshot.png")
findings[0].status = FindingStatus.IN_PROGRESS
print(f"Readiness after first task: {tracker.readiness_score()}%")
# Still 0% — finding not yet VERIFIED
A finding is not closed when the remediation task is marked complete. It is closed when the mock auditor re-verifies that the specific gap no longer exists:

def re_verify_finding(
    finding: MockAuditFinding,
    verifier: str,
    re_verification_notes: str,
    evidence_key: str,
) -> bool:
    """
    Marks the finding as VERIFIED if all tasks are complete and
    re-verification notes confirm the gap is closed.
    Returns True if verification succeeded.
    """
    if not all(t.completed for t in finding.remediation_tasks):
        incomplete = [t.task_id for t in finding.remediation_tasks if not t.completed]
        print(f"Cannot verify: tasks still open: {incomplete}")
        return False

    finding.status = FindingStatus.VERIFIED
    finding.verified_at = date.today()
    finding.notes += (
        f"\n[{date.today()}] Re-verified by {verifier}: {re_verification_notes} "
        f"(evidence: {evidence_key})"
    )
    return True
A finding that appeared in a prior mock audit or real audit and recurs is flagged as a

. Repeat findings attract disproportionate auditor attention because they signal that management responses were either ineffective or not implemented. Track repeat status from the first day the finding is raised:

content: : compare each new finding's control ID and condition against the prior audit's finding log. An exact match on control + root cause = repeat.
: a repeat CRITICAL or HIGH finding should go to the CISO the same day it is raised in the mock audit — not when fieldwork begins.
: if the same gap reappears, the prior remediation was either incomplete or the fix regressed. Investigate why before scheduling new remediation.

content.text: Publish the readiness score daily from T-30 days to audit fieldwork. Stakeholders need a single number that summarises where the programme stands:

def readiness_dashboard(tracker: RemediationTracker) -> None:
    s = tracker.summary()
    score = s["readiness_score"]

    band = (
        "NOT READY"    if score < 60 else
        "AT RISK"      if score < 75 else
        "PROGRESSING"  if score < 90 else
        "READY"        if score < 100 else
        "AUDITABLE"
    )

    print(f"Audit Readiness Score: {score}%  [{band}]")
    print(f"  Total findings:    {s['total']}")
    print(f"  Open criticals:    {s['open_criticals']}   ← must be 0 before fieldwork")
    print(f"  Overdue findings:  {s['overdue']}")
    print(f"  Repeat findings:   {s['repeat_findings']}   ← escalate immediately")
    print(f"  By status:         {s['by_status']}")

content: : a mock audit at T-14 days leaves no time to remediate CRITICAL findings. Run at T-60 days minimum.
: mock auditors who know the control owners may rate findings conservatively to avoid conflict. Assign the mock auditor role to someone outside the team that owns the controls.
: marking a task complete without the mock auditor confirming the gap is closed means the real audit will surface it again.
: if the prior audit's findings are not loaded into the tracker, repeat findings go undetected until the real auditor raises the issue.
: a score that lives only in a spreadsheet does not drive urgency. Publish it daily to the CISO and risk committee so stakeholders can see the trajectory.

content.text: Mock audits convert the audit preparation process from reactive to proactive. Apply real audit methodology — PBC requests, walk-throughs, evidence sampling — at T-60 days so findings can be remediated before fieldwork begins. Structure every finding with condition, criteria, cause, effect, and recommendation so the path to closure is unambiguous. Track remediation tasks to completion, re-verify each finding before marking it closed, and publish a daily readiness score so the risk committee can see whether the programme is on track. Treat repeat findings as the highest-priority signal: if the same gap recurs, the programme has a systemic problem, not a one-time evidence gap.
